role_adherence_notes.linguistic: Focused on Hangul script and romanization style.
role_adherence_notes.landscape: Analyzed terrain, settlement patterns, and vegetation.
role_adherence_notes.botanics: Identified specific pine species and shrubbery.
role_adherence_notes.regulatory: Discussed road markings, signs, and infrastructure standards.
role_adherence_notes.meta: Synthesized cross-domain cues like the tractor brand and pole style.

visual_consistency_notes.landscape: Rural setting, hills, and pines match description.
visual_consistency_notes.botanics: Pine trees and white shrubs are present.
visual_consistency_notes.regulatory: Road markings and pole stripes are visible.
visual_consistency_notes.meta: LS Tractor and pole details are visible.

verdict.overall_verdict_notes: The agents unanimously identified South Korea based on strong, mutually reinforcing evidence (Hangul script, specific road infrastructure, local tractor brand). The judge correctly followed Path A (consensus) and produced a final prediction that matches the ground truth.
verdict.region_narrowing_notes: Perfect consensus across all five agents on the region (East Asia/South Korea). The judge correctly identified this and skipped the region hypothesis phase.
verdict.hypothesis_pool_notes: The agents proposed South Korea as the primary candidate. While the pool technically only lists the winner, the evidence provided (Hangul, LS Tractor) makes South Korea the only visually plausible candidate, rendering a diverse pool unnecessary.
